title: Easy Creamy Russian Beef Pilaf
description: A winey and creamy Russian dish featuring ground cardamom, perfect for a family dinner, this low-sodium friendly recipe is kid-friendly and delicious.
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and cook the ground beef until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the ground cardamom and cook for 1 minute.
Add the white wine to the skillet and bring to a boil, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Add the beef broth to the skillet and bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in the heavy cream and cook until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Add the cooked rice to the skillet and stir to combine with the beef and sauce.
Season the pilaf with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
